objective: Harden existing hook guardrails by centralizing Bash write classification, preventing read-only command false positives, making branch parsing heredoc-safe, and blocking force-push commands locally.
scope_boundary: Fix hook/classifier behavior for observed session guardrail failures.
Preserve existing plan gate, SoM write-boundary, branch-switch, and issue-worktree semantics.
Add focused tests for false-positive and true-positive command shapes.
out_of_scope: Implementing supervisor, merge-flow, consumer verifier, or SQL drift probe changes from sibling sprint issues.
Replacing local hooks with a new enforcement layer.
Changing repository force-push policy beyond enforcing the existing prohibition.
research_summary: Specialist research found the plan gate is already wired, but raw regex write detection in schema guard and plan preflight misclassifies quoted awk/jq comparisons. Branch-switch guard is not heredoc-aware and force-push is not locally blocked. The recommended path is to fix existing hooks and add regression tests.

sprints.name: Hook command classifier and force-push guard
sprints.goal: Classify Bash mutation intent accurately and enforce no-force-push policy in the existing hook layer.
tasks: Centralize Bash write-target detection in scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py.
Update hooks/schema-guard.sh to consume the shared classifier instead of duplicating raw regex detection.
Make hooks/branch-switch-guard.sh ignore heredoc bodies for policy matching.
Add regression coverage for read-only awk/jq commands, real writes, heredoc branch text, and force-push forms.
acceptance_criteria: Read-only awk/jq comparison commands pass schema guard and plan preflight.
Real Bash file writes still route to missing-plan or SoM write-boundary blocks.
Heredoc bodies containing branch-switch text do not trigger branch-switch blocks.
git push --force, git push -f, git push --force-with-lease, and force refspec pushes are blocked.
Focused tests and shell syntax checks pass.

specialist_reviews.focus: Existing hook enforcement and command parsing
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended fixing schema guard, plan preflight, and branch guard in place; force-push lacks local enforcement.
Recommended centralizing write detection in plan preflight, removing duplicate schema-guard regex, and adding heredoc plus force-push tests.

alternate_model_review.summary: Parallel specialist research accepted the focused guardrail repair plan and found no need for a redundant enforcement layer.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implementation may update only the hook/classifier files, focused tests, and issue #538 governance evidence listed in the execution scope.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement hook classifier and force-push guard, then run focused validation.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Implementation stays inside allowed write paths.
Self-learning/session-error evidence is recorded before closeout.

material_deviation_rules: If implementation requires new files outside allowed_write_paths, stop and update the execution scope before editing.
If force-push exceptions are required, stop and create an issue-backed exception instead of adding a broad bypass.
If a broader shell parser is needed, record the limitation and split it into a follow-up issue.
